MEDEC paper published Dec 2024 (post Oct 22 checkpoint) lists "the latest model (≈175B parameters)" for Claude 3.5 Sonnet — almost certainly refers to the then-current claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022 checkpoint. Same 175B figure cited by 36kr/Musk-leak coverage

10T figure carried by "Claude Mythos 5: The First 10-Trillion-Parameter Model" (Medium, Analyst Uttam, Apr 5 2026), which quotes "a staggering 10 trillion parameters" and attributes it to "circulating reports and leaked materials" from the late-March-2026 Anthropic CMS misconfiguration exposure. Not an official disclosure — Anthropic has not confirmed the exact count — so param_disclosure=estimated rather than leaked. active=total pending any MoE disclosure.

params undisclosed; successor to GPT-4 with 128k context and vision

xAI did not disclose parameters for Grok-1.5; blog describes it as an upgrade to Grok-1 with 128K context and improved reasoning on math/code; TechCrunch supporting_url confirms the March 28 2024 announcement date and 128K context. x.ai/news/* pages systematically 403 to automated fetchers (Cloudflare bot-block) but resolve to browsers.

xAI first-generation multimodal model (vision-enabled Grok-1.5). Never released publicly beyond early testers per secondary coverage. SiliconANGLE supporting_url confirms vision capabilities (documents, diagrams, screenshots, photographs) and the April 12 2024 announcement date. context_window left blank — neither cited URL gives a specific number for 1.5V; inherited-from-1.5 framing is inference, not disclosure. Parameters undisclosed.

xAI Grok-2 beta release on X platform; TechCrunch supporting_url (dated Aug 13 2024) confirms "xAI launched Grok-2 and Grok-2 mini in beta today" and highlights image generation via Flux at launch (image understanding / vision input was added later in October 2024, so cap_vision=false at release). Parameters undisclosed. context_window left blank — TechCrunch does not give a token number for Grok-2, and no contemporaneous fetchable source disclosed the exact limit at Aug 13 release (the API-era grok-2-1212 variant documents 131,072 but shipped in Dec 2024).

Smaller sibling of Grok-2 announced the same day; TechCrunch supporting_url (dated Aug 13 2024) confirms Grok-2 mini shipped alongside Grok-2 in beta. Parameters undisclosed. context_window left blank — TechCrunch does not give a token number, and no contemporaneous fetchable source disclosed the exact Grok-2 mini context at release. Vision input not available at release (added October 2024 for Grok-2 family).

Industry estimate from AppLabx analysis and artsmart.ai: Grok-3 "2.7 trillion parameters" trained on 12.8T tokens. AppLabx supporting_url also confirms Feb 17 2025 launch and multimodal architecture (cap_vision). active_params set equal to total per 'other' architecture default; LifeArchitect and ybuild.ai separately estimate MoE with ~300-600B active but AppLabx does not confirm MoE

Reasoning beta sharing Grok-3 base model weights: AppLabx analysis gives "2.7 trillion parameters" for Grok-3 family, plus confirmation of Feb 17 2025 launch and multimodal support. Think variant is a separately-announced reasoning-mode checkpoint of the same base. Parameters never officially disclosed by xAI. LifeArchitect/ybuild separately estimate MoE with ~300-600B active but the cited source does not confirm MoE

Lighter faster sibling of Grok-3 announced the same day. TechCrunch supporting_url calls it 'a faster, lighter variant for quicker responses'. Parameters undisclosed. context_window left blank — TechCrunch does not give a number for Grok-3 mini and the xAI blog is Cloudflare-blocked for automated verification. Non-reasoning variant — reasoning 'Think' mode is a separate row.

Separately-announced reasoning beta of Grok-3 mini; TechCrunch supporting_url groups it with Grok-3 (Think) as 'versions that deliberate through problems before responding, similar to OpenAI's o3-mini'. Parameters undisclosed. context_window left blank — neither cited URL gives a specific token count for the mini-Think variant.

xAI flagship reasoning model. Data Science Dojo supporting_url reports "Grok 4 boasts around 1.7 trillion parameters" (also echoed by deeplearning.ai The Batch as "Mixture of experts transformer, 1.7 trillion parameters" and whitex.ai as "1.7 trillion parameters in a dense transformer stack"). Same source confirms July 2025 release, 256k API context, text+image reasoning, and function calling. Architecture kept 'other' — Data Science Dojo says "modular architecture" not MoE/dense, and other secondaries disagree; active_params set equal to total per absence of confirmed MoE.

Multi-agent variant of Grok-4 (same base weights per agent). Data Science Dojo supporting_url describes "Grok 4 Heavy: A multi-agent architecture for complex collaborative reasoning" and states Grok 4 base has "around 1.7 trillion parameters" — applied per-agent here since the Heavy variant spawns instances of the same model. Same source confirms July 2025 release, image reasoning (cap_vision), function calling, and $300/month SuperGrok Heavy tier. context_window blank; Data Science Dojo cites 256k API for base Grok-4 but does not state Heavy's own figure separately.

Unified togglable-reasoning model with 2M-token context window; Simon Willison supporting_url confirms 'same model weights handle reasoning and non-reasoning based on a parameter passed to the model' (so cap_reasoning=true — reasoning is a native toggleable mode), '2 million token context window', and end-to-end tool-use RL training. xAI blog dates release Sep 19 2025; freely available to all users including free tier. Parameters undisclosed.

Frontier model stressing conversational intelligence, emotional understanding, and reduced hallucinations. Better Stack supporting_url confirms November 17 2025 launch, multi-step 'Thinking' reasoning mode, and tool-calling Agent Tools API (web search, X search, code execution, retrieval). xAI reported LMArena Text Arena #1 (1483 Elo) at release. Parameters undisclosed; context 256K for main variant per multiple secondary sources.

Cost-efficient agentic sibling to Grok-4.1 shipped alongside it; Better Stack supporting_url confirms 2-million token context window and Agent Tools API for orchestrating external tools. Parameters undisclosed.

Public-beta 4-agent successor to Grok-4.1. Natural20 supporting_url quotes Elon Musk directly: "This is just our V8 small foundation model, so 500B params" — the self-described "small" variant at Feb 17 2026 launch; larger Grok 4.20 variants still training. Natural20 also confirms Feb 17 2026 date, 4-agent multi-agent architecture, and 256K (up to 2M in agentic mode) context. Musk's same 0.5T figure is what anchored the April 2026 Anthropic leak chain (Sonnet 4.5=1T, Opus 4.6=5T per tweet math). Architecture kept 'other' — third-party analyses describe a shared backbone but no single cited source confirms MoE with per-token active counts.

35B dense model, open-weights under CC-BY-NC (non-commercial) + Cohere Labs Acceptable Use Policy. Param count and 128K context on HF model card (supporting_url). Cohere blog announcement covers date and RAG/tool-use positioning.

104B dense flagship, open-weights under CC-BY-NC. Purpose-built for tool use / multi-step function calling and RAG with citations; launched first on Microsoft Azure. Param count and 128K context on HF model card (supporting_url).

Smallest and final model in Cohere's R series; 7B dense model, open-weights under CC-BY-NC. 128K context via sliding-window + global attention. RAG-optimized with inline citations, tool use, 23 languages. Param count and license on HF model card (supporting_url).

111B dense flagship for agentic enterprise tasks; requires only 2 GPUs (A100/H100). 256K context native. Open-weights under CC-BY-NC. 150% higher throughput vs Command R+ 08-2024. Param count and context on HF card (supporting_url). Cohere docs changelog for announcement; model ID "03-2025" embeds release month.

Multimodal extension of Command A with SigLIP2-patch16-512 vision encoder. 112B total params (111B text + vision encoder). Supports up to 20 images per request at 3328 tokens per image. Open-weights under CC-BY-NC. Param count, context, and vision architecture on HF card (supporting_url).

178B dense model launched via AI21 Studio API; 3B params more than GPT-3 175B and the largest publicly-accessible LM at its announcement. 76 layers, novel 250K-token vocabulary with multi-word tokens. Context window 2048 tokens per technical paper (supporting_url).

AI21 did not disclose Jurassic-2 parameter counts. J2 family (Large/Grande/Jumbo + instruct-tuned) launched together on Mar 9 2023; Jumbo is flagship. Claimed 30% faster than J1 with zero-shot instruction-following and 6+ non-English languages. Grande and Large variants not added as separate rows — identical-unknown parameter status makes them redundant to the flagship row. Architecture (dense vs MoE) not confirmed in cited URL; marked 'other' per conservatism pattern.

Inflection did not disclose parameters for Inflection-1, the LLM powering Pi. Announced as "best model in its compute class" — compute comparable to GPT-3.5. Outperformed GPT-3.5, LLaMA, Chinchilla, PaLM-540B on MMLU per Inflection's benchmarks (72.7%). Context window not disclosed. Architecture (dense vs MoE) not confirmed in cited URL; marked 'other' per conservatism pattern.

Inflection did not disclose parameters. Trained on 5,000 NVIDIA H100 GPUs in fp8 mixed precision (~10^25 FLOPs — same compute class as Google's PaLM 2 Large). Outperformed PaLM 2 on MMLU/TriviaQA/HellaSwag/GSM8k per Inflection's benchmarks. Context window not disclosed. Architecture (dense vs MoE) not confirmed in cited URL; marked 'other' per conservatism pattern.

Inflection did not disclose parameters. Claimed "94% average performance of GPT-4 on only 40% of training FLOPs". Added real-time web search, coding and math improvements. Hosted on Microsoft Azure. Shortly after release most of Inflection's leadership moved to Microsoft.

21B dense multimodal model (text + image + video + audio inputs); announced alongside Reka Edge (7B) as closed-weight models accessible via Reka Playground / API. Tech report (supporting_url) confirms 21B param count and multimodal capabilities.

7B dense multimodal model (text + image + video + audio inputs); announced same-day as Reka Flash (21B) as Reka's compact tier. Closed weights at Feb 2024 launch. Tech report (supporting_url) confirms 7B param count and multimodal capabilities.

Reka did not disclose Core's parameter count (Flash=21B and Edge=7B were disclosed, Core intentionally withheld). Frontier-class multimodal LLM with 128K context, 32 languages, text+image+video+audio inputs. Benchmarked competitively with GPT-4V on MMMU and ranked #2 on blind multimodal human eval (ahead of Claude 3 Opus). Tech report (supporting_url) covers multimodal architecture but also doesn't disclose Core's size.

21B general-purpose reasoning model trained from scratch with synthetic + public SFT and RLOO (rule-based + model-based rewards). Apache 2.0 open-weights — Reka's first open-weights release. Extended chain-of-thought reasoning with controllable token budget. Llama-compatible format. HF card (supporting_url) confirms 21B params and Apache 2.0 license.

Amazon did not disclose Nova parameters. Text-only lowest-latency tier of the Dec 2024 re:Invent Nova family. 128K context input tokens per AWS blog. Supports 200+ languages. Tool use / function calling via Bedrock Converse API.

Amazon did not disclose Nova parameters. Multimodal (text + image + video input) low-cost tier launched at re:Invent Dec 2024. 300K context input tokens. Can process up to 30 minutes of video in a single request. 200+ languages. Tool use via Bedrock Converse API.

Amazon did not disclose Nova parameters. Multimodal (text + image + video input) mid-tier launched at re:Invent Dec 2024. 300K context input tokens. Can process codebases with 15,000+ lines. 200+ languages. Tool use via Bedrock Converse API.

Amazon did not disclose Nova parameters. Most-capable Nova tier for complex reasoning and as teacher for model distillation. 1M token context window. Multimodal (text + image + video input) per AWS announcement. Agentic workflow support. Announced Dec 2024 "in training", released end of April 2025.

175B parameter dense decoder-only transformer; first fully-open 175B model. Trained on 180B tokens. Released under noncommercial research license. Sequence length 2048. Architecture and parameter count from arXiv paper 2205.01068.

120B parameter dense decoder-only science LLM trained on 106B tokens of scientific text (papers, textbooks, code). Released via HuggingFace under CC-BY-NC-4.0; public demo taken down after 3 days due to hallucination concerns but weights remain available. Parameter count and details from arXiv paper 2211.09085 (Galactica tech report) and HuggingFace card.
Smallest LLaMA 1 variant. 6.7B parameters per arXiv paper. Dense transformer, trained on 1T tokens. Non-commercial research license. Context window 2048 per LLaMA paper.

LLaMA 1 13B variant. Dense transformer trained on 1T tokens. Meta claimed LLaMA-13B outperformed GPT-3 175B on most benchmarks while running on a single GPU. Non-commercial research license. Context window 2048.
LLaMA 1 33B variant (technically 32.5B per paper). Dense transformer trained on 1.4T tokens. Non-commercial research license. Context window 2048.

Flagship LLaMA 1 variant (technically 65.2B per paper). Dense transformer trained on 1.4T tokens. Competitive with Chinchilla 70B and PaLM 540B at release. Non-commercial research license. Largest open-weight model released in Feb 2023 (surpassing OPT-175B for capability in the open-weight frontier via better data efficiency). Context window 2048.

Llama 2 7B base and chat variants released under Llama 2 Community License (permits commercial use). Dense transformer trained on 2T tokens. Context window 4096. Parameter count from Llama 2 paper (arXiv 2307.09288).
Llama 2 13B base and chat variants. Dense transformer trained on 2T tokens. Context window 4096. Commercial-use community license.

Llama 2 flagship. Dense transformer with grouped-query attention, trained on 2T tokens. Context window 4096. Commercial-use community license. Was the most capable open-weights model at release (pre-Falcon-180B).

Code Llama 7B — code-specialized fine-tune of Llama 2 7B. Trained on 500B tokens of code. Supports up to 100K token inputs (trained on 16K sequences). Available in base, Python-specialized, and Instruct variants. Same community license as Llama 2.

Code Llama 13B — code-specialized fine-tune of Llama 2 13B. Trained on 500B tokens of code. Supports up to 100K token inputs (trained on 16K sequences). Available in base, Python, and Instruct variants.

Code Llama 34B — largest code-specialized variant in initial Aug 2023 release. Trained on 500B tokens of code. No direct Llama 2 34B base (34B Llama 2 was not released). Supports up to 100K token inputs (trained on 16K sequences).

Code Llama 70B — largest Code Llama variant, released Jan 29 2024 (separate from the Aug 2023 7B/13B/34B batch). Trained on 1T tokens of code (more than the 500B for smaller variants). Context window 16K (trained on 16K sequences, stable to 100K). Base, Python, and Instruct variants.
Llama 3 8B instruct & base. Dense transformer with grouped-query attention, trained on 15T+ tokens. Context window 8192 tokens (8K). New tokenizer with 128K vocab. Llama 3 Community License.
Llama 3 70B flagship. Dense transformer with GQA, trained on 15T+ tokens. Context window 8192. Best-in-class open-weights model at April 2024 release (surpassing Mixtral 8x22B on most benchmarks).
Llama 3.1 8B — updated 8B with 128K context and tool use. Dense transformer. Multilingual (8 languages). Llama 3.1 Community License.
Llama 3.1 70B — updated 70B with 128K context and tool use. Dense transformer. Multilingual (8 languages).

Llama 3.1 405B — Meta's flagship and first open-weights model to genuinely contest the overall frontier (competitive with GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet on many benchmarks). Dense transformer trained on 15T+ tokens on 16K+ H100 GPUs. Context window 128K. Native tool use (function calling). First openly available frontier-class model.
Llama 3.2 1B lightweight text-only model for edge/mobile devices. Dense transformer. 128K context. Supports tool calling. Optimized for Arm/Qualcomm/MediaTek hardware on day one.
Llama 3.2 3B lightweight text-only model for edge devices. Dense transformer. 128K context. Supports tool calling.

Llama 3.2 11B Vision — multimodal (image + text). First Meta vision-capable open-weights model. Drop-in replacement for Llama 3.1 8B text model with added vision encoder. 128K context per HF card. cap_tool_use=false: Meta's Sep 2024 blog only specifies tool calling for the 1B/3B text-only variants, not for 11B/90B vision.

Llama 3.2 90B Vision — flagship multimodal open-weights model. Competitive with Claude 3 Haiku and GPT-4o-mini on image recognition tasks per Meta. 128K context. Built on Llama 3.1 70B with vision encoder. cap_tool_use=false: Meta's Sep 2024 blog only specifies tool calling for the 1B/3B text-only variants, not for 11B/90B vision.
Llama 3.3 70B Instruct — text-only efficiency-focused update delivering claimed 405B-level performance in a 70B package. Dense transformer. 128K context. 8 languages. Llama 3.3 Community License.

Llama 4 Scout — MoE model, 17B active / 109B total, 16 experts. First natively multimodal Llama with early-fusion text+vision. 10M token context window (industry-leading). Meta's first MoE production model. Released on llama.com and Hugging Face.

Llama 4 Maverick — MoE model, 17B active / 400B total, 128 routed experts + 1 shared expert. Natively multimodal (text + vision early fusion). Instruct model supports 1M context length. Released Apr 5 2025.

Llama 4 Behemoth — ~2T total / 288B active, 16 experts. Announced as preview / teacher model for Scout and Maverick distillation. Not publicly released as of 2026-04; training reportedly paused mid-2025. Included here as the announced flagship of the Llama 4 family though weights are not available (open_weights=false reflects lack of public release).
DeepSeek LLM 7B (base + chat) — dense LLaMA-style transformer with Multi-Head Attention, trained on 2T tokens English+Chinese. 4K context. DeepSeek License (commercial use allowed).

DeepSeek LLM 67B (base + chat) — dense transformer with Grouped-Query Attention, trained on 2T tokens English+Chinese. 4K context. Outperformed Llama 2 70B on reasoning/coding/math/Chinese benchmarks per README.

DeepSeek-Coder 33B Instruct — flagship of family (1.3B/5.7B/6.7B/33B). Dense transformer trained on 2T tokens (87% code + 13% NL, 87 programming languages). 16K context via fill-in-the-middle extended pre-training. Code-specialized.

DeepSeekMoE 16B — architecture pivot to MoE with fine-grained expert specialization + shared expert isolation. 16.4B total / 2.8B active. Comparable to Llama2 7B at ~40% compute. Prototype for V2/V3 sparse architecture.

DeepSeek-V2 — 236B/21B MoE with Multi-head Latent Attention (MLA) + DeepSeekMoE sparse routing. 128K context. 42.5% lower training cost vs DeepSeek 67B, 93.3% KV cache reduction. Largest open MoE at May 2024.

DeepSeek-Coder-V2 (236B/21B MoE flagship; 16B/2.4B Lite also released). Further pre-trained from V2 intermediate checkpoint with +6T code tokens. 338 programming languages (up from 86). 128K context. Claimed comparable to GPT-4-Turbo on code benchmarks.

DeepSeek-V2.5 — merge of DeepSeek-V2-0628 + DeepSeek-Coder-V2-0724, combining general + coding capabilities. 236B/21B MoE. HF model card's vLLM quickstart example sets max_model_len=8192 — using that as the sourceable context window since the architectural max is not stated in either cited URL (V2's 128K does not automatically carry over per source-substantiation rule).

DeepSeek-V3 — 671B/37B MoE with MLA + auxiliary-loss-free load balancing + multi-token prediction. Trained on 14.8T tokens using FP8 mixed precision. 128K context. Open-weights flagship contesting GPT-4o / Claude 3.5 Sonnet-1022 class. cap_tool_use=false: neither cited URL explicitly confirms function calling at release (was added formally in V3.1).

DeepSeek-R1 — first open-weights reasoning model at o1-class performance. 671B/37B MoE (same architecture as V3), post-trained via pure reinforcement learning with <think> tag chain-of-thought. Published in Nature (Sep 2025). 128K context.

DeepSeek-V3.1 — hybrid thinking/non-thinking MoE. 671B per primary HF model card (supporting AnalyticsVidhya article cites 685B including MTP auxiliary module; keeping 671B to match primary). 37B active. 128K context. FP8 UE8M0 quantization. Toggleable reasoning via chat template thinking flag. Tool calling in non-thinking mode; code+search agent support.

DeepSeek-V3.2-Exp — experimental release testing DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) on top of MLA. 671B MoE with 256 experts. Inherits V3.1 hybrid thinking + tool calling. Config references config_671B_v3.2.json on HF.

DeepSeek-V3.2 — official successor to V3.2-Exp. 671B/37B MoE with DSA sparse attention. Integrates 'thinking with tools' (first model to unify reasoning with tool use). V3.2-Speciale is an API-only reasoning-first variant (not included as separate row). 128K context. Date matches arxiv submission 2512.02556 v1 (Dec 2 2025); HF collection shows 'Updated Dec 1 2025' which the verifier flagged as collection metadata, not model release.

Qwen-7B — first Qwen release (Aug 3 2023 on ModelScope + HF). Dense transformer (RoPE, SwiGLU, RMSNorm) trained on 2.4T tokens. Initial context 2048, extended to 8192 in Sep 2023 retraining. Tongyi Qianwen License (commercial use with free application).

Qwen-14B — 40-layer dense transformer trained on 3T+ tokens. 2048 native context (extendable to 8K via NTK+LogN+window attention). Tongyi Qianwen License. Supporting_url = GitHub README for release-date timeline (HF card does not state Sept 25 exactly).
Qwen-72B — 80-layer dense transformer trained on 3T+ tokens. 32K native context. Tongyi Qianwen License. Largest Qwen 1 model; released same day as Qwen-1.8B.

Qwen1.5-72B — sweeping refresh of Qwen family released Feb 4 2024 across 0.5B/1.8B/4B/7B/14B/32B/72B sizes (110B came later April 2024). Uniform 32K context. Function calling demonstrated in blog. Tongyi Qianwen License for 72B.

Qwen1.5-MoE-A2.7B — first Qwen MoE. 14.3B total / 2.7B activated. 64 experts (60 routing + 4 shared), 4-of-60 routed active. Matches Qwen1.5-7B quality at ~1/3 activated params. Apache 2.0. 8K context per HF config.

Qwen2-72B — 73B dense transformer with GQA. 128K context via YaRN. Pretrained on 7T+ tokens (Qwen2 family). Tongyi Qianwen License (smaller Qwen2 models are Apache 2.0). Function calling officially supported per Qwen2 ecosystem.
Qwen2-57B-A14B — second Qwen MoE, upcycled from dense. 57B/14B active. 64K context. Apache 2.0. Released alongside Qwen2-72B.

Qwen2-VL-72B — ~72B LLM + 600M ViT = ~73B total. Dense multimodal with Naive Dynamic Resolution + M-RoPE. Understands 20+ min videos. 72B initially API-only at announcement (2B/7B open-weight; 72B open-weight released later). cap_tool_use reflects visual-agent/device-control capability. 32K context per HF card.

Qwen2.5-72B — 73B dense flagship of Qwen2.5 family. 128K context. Pretrained on 18T tokens. Function calling via Qwen-Agent and HF tooling. Qwen License (72B); smaller sizes Apache 2.0 except 3B (Qwen Research).
Qwen2.5-Coder-32B — dense code-specialized flagship. Trained on 5.5T tokens. 128K context (32K default). Apache 2.0. Matches GPT-4o on EvalPlus, LiveCodeBench, BigCodeBench per Qwen blog.

QwQ-32B-Preview — first open-weight reasoning model approaching o1-mini performance. Dense, based on Qwen2.5-32B. Apache 2.0. 32K context. Known limitations: language switching, recursive loops per HF card.

Qwen2.5-VL-72B — ~73B dense multimodal. M-RoPE + dynamic FPS sampling. Visual agent with computer/phone use capabilities. 1hr+ video understanding. Qwen License. Open-weight at launch (unlike Qwen2-VL-72B). 32K context native.
QwQ-32B — full release of Qwen reasoning model, 32B dense, RL-scaled from Qwen2.5-32B. 128K context (YaRN). Apache 2.0. Near-DeepSeek-R1 performance at ~20x fewer parameters.

Qwen3-235B-A22B — flagship Qwen3 MoE. 235B total / 22B active (8-of-128 experts). 94 layers, GQA 64Q/4KV. Hybrid thinking/non-thinking mode. Apache 2.0. 128K context via YaRN (32K native). Competitive with DeepSeek-R1, o1, o3-mini, Grok-3, Gemini-2.5-Pro per Qwen blog.
Qwen3-32B — dense flagship sibling to Qwen3-235B-A22B MoE. Hybrid reasoning (thinking + non-thinking). 128K context via YaRN. Apache 2.0. Performance matches Qwen2.5-72B-Base per Qwen blog.
Qwen3-30B-A3B — small MoE with 30.5B total / 3.3B active (8-of-128 experts). Outcompetes QwQ-32B with 10x fewer activated params. Hybrid thinking. 128K context via YaRN. Apache 2.0.

Qwen3-Coder-480B-A35B-Instruct — flagship open agentic coding MoE. 480B total / 35B active. 256K native context (1M with YaRN). Apache 2.0. SOTA among open models on Agentic Coding / Browser-Use / Tool-Use per Qwen blog. Non-thinking Instruct variant (no reasoning mode at this release).

Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B — first Qwen native omni-modal model. Thinker-Talker architecture for unified text/image/audio/video processing with real-time speech output. MoE 30B/3B active. 119 text / 19 ASR / 10 TTS languages. Apache 2.0. 32K context native (65K multi-GPU). SOTA on 22/36 audio-video benchmarks. supporting_url blanked — the previous MarkTechPost link pointed to a different later model (Qwen3.5-Omni, Mar 2026).

Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B — flagship multimodal MoE. 235B/22B active. 256K native context, 1M with YaRN. 2-hour video understanding with 99.5% needle-in-haystack accuracy per unite.ai. Apache 2.0. Thinking variants available. Date matches GitHub's 'Release Date: November 27 2025 (technical report publication)' — supporting unite.ai says 'initially launched September 2025' but does not pin a specific day; using the Nov 27 date that both cited sources literally confirm.

Qwen3-Max-Preview (Sept 5 2025) / Qwen3-Max GA (Sept 24 2025) — first Qwen model exceeding 1T parameters. Sparse MoE. API-only (Qwen Chat + Alibaba Cloud Model Studio); no open weights. Instruct + Thinking variants. Active param count not publicly disclosed. total=1T reflects the '>1T' lower bound stated by Qwen team. context_window blanked: the verifier could not confirm a specific value from the MarkTechPost primary (it mentions the model but not the context size in the reachable body) and the x.com supporting URL is Cloudflare-blocked.

Qwen3.5 (Qwen3.5-397B-A17B) — hybrid Gated Delta Networks linear attention + sparse MoE. 397B total / 17B active (very sparse ratio). Natively multimodal (text + vision + video); audio modality is handled by separate Qwen3.5-Omni (Mar 2026). Open-weight per CNBC / VentureBeat; hosted Qwen3.5-Plus variant uses 1M default context. 201 languages. Tool use + thinking mode.

Qwen3.6-35B-A3B — small sparse MoE positioned for agentic coding. 35B total / 3B active. 256K context per GitHub repo. Apache 2.0. Not marketed as code-specialized (no '-Coder' suffix) so cap_code_specialized=false per PLAN convention. 'Preserve thinking' reasoning feature inherited from 3.6 family. cap_vision=true flipped from the researcher's conservative false after verifier found the aibase supporting URL explicitly states 'Visual perception with a RefCOCO score of 92.0' plus 'multimodal metrics'.

Qwen3.6-27B — dense flagship-level coding model (27B). Apache 2.0. Released via GitHub / HF / ModelScope. Two days before the 2026-04-24 cutoff. cap_vision=true and cap_video=true flipped from the researcher's conservative false after verifier found the BuildFastWithAI supporting URL explicitly states 'Multimodal support (text, image, and video inputs)'.

340M parameters per BERT paper (24 layers, 1024 hidden, 16 heads); HF card gives 336M including embeddings — same model, standard citation is 340M. Weights released at google-research/bert under Apache 2.0. Context window = 512 tokens max sequence length (supporting_url HF card confirms).

11B flagship of the T5 family (unified text-to-text encoder-decoder). Released under Apache 2.0 by Google Research. Context window = 512 tokens (standard T5 pretraining sequence length, confirmed in supporting_url HF card and widely in T5 documentation).

8.3B dense GPT-2-style decoder-only transformer. Paper states: 'We open source our code along with the training and evaluation pipelines at github.com/NVIDIA/Megatron-LM'. Training used sequences of 1024 subword units (supporting_url confirms). Briefly the largest dense autoregressive LM (Sep 2019 → Feb 2020). open_weights=false and frontier_open_at_release=false: both cited URLs confirm only training code was open-sourced; neither explicitly states pretrained 8.3B checkpoints were publicly downloadable, so defaulting to false per source-substantiation rule.

17B dense decoder-only transformer (78 layers, hidden size 4256, 28 heads). Trained with sequences of 1024 tokens. Closed-weights: Microsoft released only a private demo to select academic users — weights never publicly downloadable. Largest announced LM in Feb 2020 until superseded by GPT-3 (May 2020).

HF model card states 1.6T total / 49B activated MoE; MIT license; 1M context; Hybrid Attention (CSA+HCA); 32T+ pretraining tokens. supporting_url is DeepSeek API function-calling guide, supports cap_tool_use.
